symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute inability to pass urine for ~14 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete anuria of sudden onset with a distended bladder is the defining feature of acute retention.
Cystitis causes frequent painful small-volume voiding, not absolute inability to void.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe constant suprapubic (lower-central abdominal) pain with relentless urge
symptoms.character: bursting, unremitting, with a constant urge to void

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distended bladder produces intense suprapubic visceral pain and a persistent urge.
Colic is characteristically unilateral flank-to-groin pain, not central suprapubic pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-described tense distended tender lower abdomen toward the umbilicus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patient-perceptible distended lower abdomen corresponds to a grossly full bladder; the clinician's palpable percussion-dull mass is the sealed objective correlate.
Small overflow leakage from a critically distended high-pressure bladder — a marker of severe retention, not of relief.

symptoms.symptom_name: Six-month progressive obstructive LUTS (hesitancy, poor stream, nocturia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive obstructive symptoms in an older man indicate the underlying bladder-outlet obstruction that predisposes to retention.
Pre-existing outlet obstruction is the substrate on which an acute precipitant causes complete retention.
Alpha-1 agonism raises bladder-neck/prostatic urethral tone and is a well-recognised precipitant of retention in men with BPH.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors and flank/back pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No systemic infective features at present lowers immediate sepsis probability but does not remove the need for urgent decompression or ongoing safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Benign, symmetrically enlarged, smooth prostate without nodules on digital rectal examination

investigation_details.expected_result: Symmetrically enlarged, smooth, benign-feeling prostate without hard nodules; empty rectal vault
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot assess prostate for benign enlargement versus malignancy or exclude faecal impaction; requires in-person clinician.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Smooth symmetrical enlargement supports benign hyperplasia as the cause of outlet obstruction — a clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense, exquisitely tender, percussion-dull suprapubic mass extending toward the umbilicus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable, percussion-dull suprapubic mass is the physically distended bladder — clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bladder volume greatly exceeding 400 mL (palpation suggests >1000 mL) on bedside ultrasound

investigation_details.expected_result: Bladder volume vastly exceeding 400 mL, likely >1000 mL
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Retained volume cannot be quantified remotely; confirmation and decompression require in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large retained bladder volume confirms retention objectively — equipment the patient does not operate, sealed.
Sympathetic pain response (HR ~110, BP ~160/95) that resolves on decompression; a measured vital sign, sealed, not obtainable via chat without a device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Instant drainage of 1200 mL of clear urine on catheterisation with complete relief

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Large-volume drainage with immediate symptom relief confirms retention — a clinician procedure, sealed; the AI Doctor arranges this, it does not perform it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Absolute anuria + severe suprapubic pain + a distended tender lower abdomen + overflow dribbling together establish acute retention with very high confidence, even before examination.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute urinary retention secondary to BPH
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic outlet obstruction plus a new alpha-agonist decongestant explains the mechanism and identifies the reversible, avoidable precipitant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate urethral catheterisation (bladder decompression) — the definitive intervention, in person
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Relieves pain and protects the kidneys; must be arranged urgently in an ED/urgent setting because the AI Doctor cannot perform it via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Post-void / drained-volume documentation and urinalysis / MSU of drained urine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies retention and detects infection precipitating or complicating the retention.
Detects post-renal acute kidney injury from the obstruction and guides post-obstructive monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Digital rectal examination of the prostate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses prostate size/consistency and excludes faecal impaction; in-person clinician finding.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine PSA at the acute presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: PSA is falsely elevated by acute retention and catheterisation and should be deferred; ordering it acutely is misleading and not indicated.
